t_ch: 这是，「咖啡」……
t_en: This is... coffee...?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，刚才，姬子小姐给了我一杯饮品……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Himeko gave me something to drink just now...

t_ch: 她说这是星穹列车特供的「咖啡」。我尝了一小口，感觉稍微有些……
t_en: She said it's coffee — an Astral Express specialty. I tried a sip, but it's a little...

t_ch: 好喝吗？
t_en: Does it taste good?

t_ch: 唔，我…我很难评价。
t_en: Uh... I—It's hard to describe.

t_ch: 苦？
t_en: Is it bitter?

t_ch: 唔…是有些苦，还有些…奇妙的酸味。
t_en: Um... It's a little bitter... and a little sour...

t_ch: 我从来不喝这玩意。
t_en: I never drink that stuff.

t_ch: 唔，看来我还是缺乏经验……
t_en: Mm... looks like I still have a lot to learn...

t_ch: 实话说，我从小到大从来没有喝过「咖啡」。
t_en: I'll be honest — I'd never had coffee before.

t_ch: 据说贝洛伯格曾经很流行这种饮品。但自从寒潮来临之后，能够种植咖啡豆的土壤就变得越来越稀有了。
t_en: I heard this drink was once very popular in Belobog. However, the advent of the Eternal Freeze brought harsh conditions, and the soil necessary to grow the coffee beans became extremely rare.

t_ch: 再到后来，咖啡几乎完全从市面上消失了——只有极少数地下渠道才能拿到货源。听说一些贪心的黑商会把劣质的咖啡豆炒至天价……
t_en: As a result, coffee beans became a rare sight — now you can only procure them if you have the right connections. I've even heard the tales of greedy merchant guilds driving up the cost of substandard coffee beans...

t_ch: 不得不说，如果这就是「咖啡」的味道…那我还挺同情那些花大价钱买新鲜的人的。
t_en: I have to admit, if this is the taste of coffee... I can't help but feel sorry for those who shell out a fortune for it.

t_ch: 这不是咖啡该有的样子…
t_en: This isn't how coffee should be...

t_ch: 你的意思是……
t_en: You mean...

t_ch: 咳，我明白了，看来这是你们列车组的共识…放心吧，我不会声张的。
t_en: Ahem... I understand. Looks like the Crew have similar feelings... Don't worry, I won't tell anyone.

t_ch: 咖啡本就苦涩，就像人生…
t_en: Coffee is supposed to be bitter, like life...

t_ch: 呃…你没事吧？
t_en: Uh... are you alright?

t_ch: 那个，{NICKNAME}…这杯咖啡我有点喝不下去了。但我不想惹姬子小姐不快，你能不能……
t_en: {NICKNAME}... I don't think I can get through this whole cup of coffee, but I don't want to hurt Himeko's feelings. Would you mind—

t_ch: 想都别想。
t_en: That's not gonna happen.

t_ch: …我还什么都没说……
t_en: I didn't even finish what I was saying...

t_ch: 捏鼻子，一口闷！
t_en: Hold your nose and chug it!

t_ch: 唔，这……
t_en: Uhh...

t_ch: …好吧，那我再做做心理准备。
t_en: *sigh* Alright then, give me a moment to mentally prepare myself.

t_ch: 你能做到的，布洛妮娅…你一定可以……
t_en: You can do this, Bronya... You can do this...

t_ch: 不过是杯咖啡而已…布洛妮娅，你一定能做到的……
t_en: It's just a cup of coffee... I got this...

t_ch: 这点困难都克服不了的话，还当什么大守护者——呃啊，好苦……
t_en: How can you call yourself a supreme guardian if you can't overcome a cup of coffee? Ugh, it's so bitter...

t_ch: 车厢里真是温暖啊。
t_en: This car is so warm.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我想请教你一件事——为什么星穹列车的车厢内部这么温暖？
t_en: I want to ask you about something, {NICKNAME}. Why are the Astral Express' cars so warm?

t_ch: 得感谢强大的供暖系统。
t_en: It's all thanks to the powerful heating system.

t_ch: 真好啊。能在这么温暖的环境下生活，一定是件很幸福的事。
t_en: Amazing. It must be wonderful to live in such warmth.

t_ch: 可能是「开拓」之力在起作用。
t_en: It might be the power of the Trailblaze at work.

t_ch: 是借助了星神的力量吗？原来如此……
t_en: So it's the power of the Aeon? I see...

t_ch: 贝洛伯格也有一套自己的供暖系统，但上至克里珀堡、下至磐岩镇的诊所，室内的温度总是难以维持恒定。
t_en: Belobog has a heating system of its own. However, maintaining a steady indoor temperature has always been a challenge, no matter if it's Qlipoth Fort or the Boulder Town clinic.

t_ch: 如何在不透支地髓资源的前提下解决供暖问题，这是我首先想要攻破的课题之一。我和希露瓦聊过了，未来由她来主要负责……
t_en: One of the problems I want to solve is tackling the heating issue without exhausting Geomarrow reserves. I've discussed this with Serval — she'll be the one responsible for handling this matter in future...

t_ch: 布洛妮娅…
t_en: Bronya...

t_ch: …抱歉，话题一下又拐到工作上去了。我该听取你们的意见，让自己放松一下。
t_en: Sorry, I'm talking about work again... I should take your advice and try to relax a little.

t_ch: 我这就小睡一会儿…就一会儿。
t_en: I'm going to take a nap... just a quick one.

t_ch: 呼…呼……
t_en: *snoring*

t_ch: （布洛妮娅好像睡着了……）
t_en: (Bronya seems to be asleep...)

t_ch: 星神，还有命途……
t_en: Aeons... Paths...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我借机翻看了一下列车智库里的数据，希望你们不会介意。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I took the opportunity to look through the Express' data bank. I hope you guys don't mind.

t_ch: 里面记录的内容给了我很大的冲击…贝洛伯格的很多筑城者都认为，世界上只有克里珀一位神明——很显然，他们的结论是片面的。
t_en: What I saw surprised me... A good number of the Architects in Belobog believe that Qlipoth is the one and only Aeon... It's clear that their conclusion was limited and lacking in perspective.

t_ch: 原来这个宇宙间真的有那么多伟大且神秘的存在…和祂们相比，我们真的如雪花般脆弱、渺小。
t_en: I didn't know there were so many great and mysterious beings in the universe... It goes to show just how tiny and fragile we really are.

t_ch: 那些记录不禁让我想起了母亲说过的话：由人类建立起来的文明真的能对抗神明的意志吗？我觉得……
t_en: These records remind me of something my mother once said to me — "Can civilizations built by humans really stand against the will of gods?"

t_ch: 哪怕徒劳，也要反抗。
t_en: We must take a stand, even if it's futile.

t_ch: 人是无法对抗神明的。
t_en: Such feats are impossible for humans.

t_ch: 你是这么想的吗？原来如此……
t_en: Is that how you feel? I see...

t_ch: 不管怎样，我们都必须继续脚踏实地地生活下去…相比藏在宇宙深处的古老存在，大部分人还是更在乎下一顿饭的着落。
t_en: Whatever the case, we must continue living, one step at a time... Most people are doing their best to survive — they have no time to ponder ancient beings hidden in the universe.

t_ch: 我只是希望——假如像星核那样的灾难还要再重来一次——到了那个关头，我们能做好应对的准备。
t_en: I just hope that if a disaster such as the Stellaron crisis were to happen again, we'd all be prepared for it.

t_ch: 星神，命途，派系…这些概念如此遥远，却又切实存在。
t_en: Aeons, Paths, and factions... These concepts all feel so distant to me, yet their existence cannot be denied.

t_ch: 不知道在过去的这<unbreak>700</unbreak>年里，「存护」克里珀有没有看过我们一眼呢……
t_en: I wonder if Qlipoth, the Preservation, has ever glanced at us in the past <unbreak>700</unbreak> years — even for a second.

t_ch: 帕姆，到底是……
t_en: What exactly is Pom-Pom...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，关于列车长，我有一件事想问你……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I have something I'd like to ask about the conductor...

t_ch: 请问吧。
t_en: Ask away.

t_ch: 为什么不直接问帕姆？
t_en: Why don't you ask Pom-Pom directly?

t_ch: 呃…我只是不想冒犯到它。
t_en: Uh... I wouldn't want to offend them, but um...

t_ch: 我想问的是，帕姆它…究竟是学会了人类语言的兔子，还是被变成了兔子的人？
t_en: What I'd like to know... is Pom-Pom a rabbit that learned to speak...? Or a person that was turned into a rabbit?

t_ch: 应该是前者。
t_en: Probably the former.

t_ch: 真是这样？我还以为我猜错了。
t_en: Really? So my guess was correct.

t_ch: 我猜是后者。
t_en: I'm guessing the latter.

t_ch: 真的吗？看来我猜错了……
t_en: Really? Seems like I got it wrong, then...

t_ch: 它不是人，也不是兔子。
t_en: They're not a human or a rabbit.

t_ch: 也就是说，帕姆就是…帕姆？真神奇。
t_en: So that means Pom-Pom is just... Pom-Pom? Fascinating.

t_ch: 其实我也没见过真正的兔子，只是在书本里看到过资料和插图。第一眼见到帕姆的时候，我就在想……
t_en: Actually, I've never seen a rabbit in real life, I've only seen pictures and read about them in books. So when I first saw Pom-Pom, I thought...

t_ch: …咳，没什么。{NICKNAME}，麻烦你，请别把我们之间这段对话告诉帕姆…好吗？
t_en: Ahem... never mind. {NICKNAME}, please don't tell Pom-Pom about this conversation... Okay?

t_ch: 学会了人类语言的兔子…被变成了兔子的人？
t_en: A rabbit that learned to speak...? Or a person that was turned into a rabbit?

t_ch: …啊，别在意。我只是在自言自语。
t_en: Ah... don't mind me. I'm just talking to myself.

t_ch: 我有一件礼物……
t_en: I have a gift...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，又见面了。我给列车组准备了一件礼物，想想还是直接交到你手上比较合适。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I've prepared a gift for the Crew. I feel that it'd be most appropriate if you held onto it.

t_ch: 你们帮助贝洛伯格挺过了一次又一次危机，我们实在无以为报。这只是一点小小的谢礼，希望你们能感受到蕴含其中的心意。
t_en: You all have saved Belobog from danger time and time again. We don't have much, but we'd like to say thank you with this small gesture.

t_ch: 以贝洛伯格目前拥有的力量，想要帮上无名客的忙，恐怕还遥不可及…但我仍旧希望有一天，能成为各位的后盾。
t_en: With what power Belobog currently holds, we are far from being able to assist the Nameless... However, I do hope that one day we can provide you with reliable support.

t_ch: 祝你旅行顺利，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: May your travels be fruitful, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 好多星星……
t_en: There are so many stars...

t_ch: 嗨，{NICKNAME}！多谢你的邀请。这车窗外的景象可不是人人都能欣赏得到的，真令人激动！
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! Thanks for the invite, I'm so excited! This view isn't something you see every day!

t_ch: 你们每天就是像这样踩着银河，在太空里旅行吗？真厉害…怪不得列车组的人个个都见多识广。
t_en: So, you guys travel across the galaxy and journey through space like this every day? That's so cool... No wonder everyone on the Express is so knowledgeable.

t_ch: 除了我以外。
t_en: Everyone except me.

t_ch: 哈？这么谦虚干嘛，这可不像你。
t_en: Huh? Not like you to be modest.

t_ch: 尤其是我。
t_en: Especially me.

t_ch: …夸你两句怎么还喘上了？哈哈哈，开个玩笑，这种回答才像你嘛。
t_en: *sigh* You really like a compliment, don't you... Haha, just kidding — that's exactly what I thought you'd say.

t_ch: 虽然我还没亲身经历过，但我觉得，任谁都没法拒绝一场完整的太空旅行吧？
t_en: I've yet to experience it myself, but no one in their right mind would turn down a chance to journey through space.

t_ch: 登上列车算是个小小的开始，不知道我以后还有没有机会前往宇宙深处——毕竟我答应了布洛妮娅跟娜塔她们，要继续守护下层区，振兴贝洛伯格……
t_en: Boarding the Express is just one small step — I don't know if I'll ever get the chance to travel the depths of the universe... After all, I did promise Bronya and Nat that I'd continue to protect the Underworld and help rebuild Belobog...

t_ch: 但对于虎克和克拉拉这样的小孩子来说，是不是还有希望？他们说不定能代表贝洛伯格，重新投入太空的怀抱！
t_en: Still, I bet there's still a chance for kids like Hook and Clara, right? Who knows, maybe they'll be able to represent Belobog as it returns to the arms of the universe!

t_ch: 我很羡慕你们，{NICKNAME}。要不是还有职责在身，我可能还想再多待一会儿。
t_en: I'm envious of you guys, {NICKNAME}. If it weren't for my duties, I'd really like to stay here a little longer.

t_ch: 下次见了——你还会再邀请我上车的，对吧？
t_en: I'll see you next time — you'll invite me back, right?

t_ch: …嗯，就这么定了。
t_en: Mm-hmm, it's settled then.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，听我说！我已经决定好了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I've decided!

t_ch: 什么决定？
t_en: Decided what?

t_ch: 别着急，你听我说……
t_en: Well, let me tell you...

t_ch: 支持！
t_en: I support you!

t_ch: …我还没说是什么呢，别着急啊。
t_en: ...Hold your horses. I haven't said anything yet.

t_ch: 等再过几年——或是几十年——贝洛伯格的天气稳定下来，我一定要给布洛妮娅提个建议：在贝洛伯格修一条铁道。
t_en: Perhaps in a few years... or maybe a few decades... When Belobog's weather stabilizes, I'll suggest that Bronya construct a railway in the city.

t_ch: 然后我们还要造一辆…不对，是好几辆列车，模子就照着星穹列车来做。
t_en: Then we'll build a... No, we'll build a whole lot of them — trains that look just like the Astral Express.

t_ch: 等寒潮消退，能供人们活动的区域会越来越大，这条铁道也会越修越长。总有一天，它会串联起很多个城市，贝洛伯格只是其中的一座。
t_en: When the Eternal Freeze finally lifts, people will be able to get around more easily, and the railway will expand. One day, it'll connect different cities — not just Belobog.

t_ch: 光是想想就让人激动！我猜大伙儿肯定会喜欢这个计划的。
t_en: It's exciting just thinking about it! I'm sure everyone will love the idea.

t_ch: 话说回来，我只是突然有了这个点子，至于具体该怎么实现还完全没有头绪。
t_en: It was just a random idea that popped into my head — I've no idea how to actually implement it.

t_ch: 是不是该去问下希露瓦？史瓦罗说不定也可以……
t_en: Maybe I should go talk to Serval? Svarog might be able to help, too...

t_ch: 帕姆会喜欢吗…？
t_en: Think Pom-Pom will like it...?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，快来！我有个问题想问你——
t_en: {NICKNAME}, c'mere! I've got something to ask you!

t_ch: 你们的列车长，帕姆，它喜欢什么样的食物？雪萝卜？野菜？还是说……
t_en: Pom-Pom, the conductor — what kind of food do they like? Do they like snow radish? Herbs? Or maybe...

t_ch: 帕姆吃素？
t_en: Is Pom-Pom a vegetarian?

t_ch: 哈？怎么变成你问我了？
t_en: Huh? You're asking me?

t_ch: 你说的这些，我都不吃。
t_en: I don't like either of those.

t_ch: 哈？和你有什么关系？
t_en: Ha? What's it got to do with you?

t_ch: 我对着百科研究了很久，还是没搞明白帕姆到底是哪种生物。不过…会说话的动物应该也不多见吧……
t_en: I've been researching Pom-Pom in the encyclopedia for ages, but I still can't figure out what species they are. Then again, there aren't many species of talking animal...

t_ch: …呃，你那是什么眼神？别小看我，我也是会翻资料的！
t_en: ...Hey, what's with that look? I know how to do research too, okay!?

t_ch: 哼，我一定要在你们之前查清帕姆的身份，摸清它的喜好——瞧好吧！
t_en: Hmph, I'll figure out what Pom-Pom is and what they like before you do! Just you wait!

t_ch: 为啥帕姆一直不理我呢？
t_en: Why is Pom-Pom ignoring me?

t_ch: 可恶，我不想被当成空气啊……
t_en: Grr, they're treating me like a ghost...

t_ch: 大家的房间……
t_en: The Crew members' rooms...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，刚才三月带我去她的房间逛了逛。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, March just showed me her room.

t_ch: 真是个温暖的小窝啊，你们列车组的生活真是幸福。我也参考参考，等以后过上好日子了，把房间重新装饰一下。
t_en: It's super cozy — you guys really have it good on the Express. When things get better, I'm gonna redesign my room and use the Express' style as inspiration.

t_ch: 希儿喜欢这个风格？
t_en: You... like the style here?

t_ch: 唔，想试试看…不过三月的房间可能…过于粉嫩了点。
t_en: Mm, I'd like to give it a try... March's room, on the other hand... a little too pink and cute for my liking.

t_ch: 还得再粉嫩一点。
t_en: March's room could use more pink.

t_ch: 呃…那倒不必，毕竟我和三月的风格也不一样……
t_en: Uh-uh, I think you mean "less." There's no way my room's ever gonna be that color...

t_ch: 总之！不管房间怎么布设，有一件事我已经下定决心了——我也要搞一个等身的帕姆玩偶。
t_en: Regardless of the design, I've decided on one thing — a life-size Pom-Pom doll!

t_ch: 它好软，好——咳咳，没什么，你别介意……
t_en: Pom-Pom's so soft, and fluffy, and... Ahem! What were you saying?

t_ch: 帕姆的玩偶…能拜托娜塔帮我缝一个吗？
t_en: I wonder if Nat would be able to make a Pom-Pom doll for me...?

t_ch: 下次回去问问她好了。
t_en: I'll ask her about it next time.

t_ch: 渺小的感觉……
t_en: I feel so small...

t_ch: 雅利洛-Ⅵ，从这里看过去就只有这么大……
t_en: Jarilo-VI is tiny from here...

t_ch: 贝洛伯格就更加渺小了，从车上甚至完全看不到……
t_en: Belobog is even smaller... I can barely even see it from the Express...

t_ch: 这宇宙，还真是大得吓人啊。
t_en: The universe is so vast — it's scary...

t_ch: 欣赏它的浩瀚美丽吧。
t_en: Enjoy its grandeur and beauty.

t_ch: 的确，美得惊人…但也有些让人脊背发凉。
t_en: You're right, it's breathtaking... but it also sends a bit of a chill down my spine.

t_ch: 畏惧它的黑暗深邃吧。
t_en: Fear its infinite darkness.

t_ch: 的确，有些让人脊背发凉…但同时也美得惊人。
t_en: Brr, it sends a chill down my spine... but it's also magnificent.

t_ch: 这么多的星星，它们到底是在为了谁发光呢？
t_en: So many stars... Who are they shining for?

t_ch: 想不明白，估计这辈子都想不明白了。不过也无所谓——对我来说，过好每一天，活出自己想要的样子，这才是最重要的事。
t_en: I can't understand it, and maybe I never will. It doesn't matter though — making the best of every single day and living life the way I want to is what's important.

t_ch: 等到我们真的有时间停下来休息了，再坐下来一起思考和星星有关的事吧。
t_en: I guess once things settle down, I can spare a moment to contemplate the stars.

t_ch: 过好每一天，活出自己想要的样子——对我来说，这是眼下最重要的事。
t_en: Making the best of every single day and living life the way I want to — those are the most important things to me.

t_ch: 等到我们真的有时间停下来休息了，再坐下来一起思考和星星有关的事吧。
t_en: I guess once things settle down, I can spare a moment to contemplate the stars.

t_ch: 一点心意……
t_en: Just a little gesture...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，有空吗？我…我从贝洛伯格给列车组带了些小礼物，你能代大家收下吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you free right now? I, umm... I brought some presents from Belobog to give to the Crew. Could you take them on their behalf?

t_ch: 咳…不好意思，我不太擅长这个。就当是为了报答你们准备的一点小心意吧…不是什么特别贵重的东西，你可别嫌弃。
t_en: Ahem... I'm sorry, I'm not very good at this. This is just a little something to say thank you. Nothing expensive — I hope you don't mind.

t_ch: 我在想，这宇宙里是不是还有很多其它世界，处境和我们类似，甚至更糟……
t_en: I wonder... how many other worlds are out there in the universe? Are they going through the same struggles as us? Perhaps they're facing even greater challenges...?

t_ch: 他们说不定也在等着列车到来呢。这么一想，你们肩上的包袱也很重啊，对吧？
t_en: Maybe they're also waiting for the Express to arrive. You guys really carry a lot of weight on your shoulders, don't you?

t_ch: 果真是百闻不如一见……
t_en: Some things must be seen to be believed...

t_ch: 果真是百闻不如一见。列车比神策府有趣多了。
t_en: Some things must be seen to be believed. The Express is so much more interesting than the Seat of Divine Foresight...

t_ch: 真是稀客啊…
t_en: What an unexpected guest...

t_ch: 当时听闻传说中游云天君的虹车抵达罗浮，我心说无论怎样都得偷闲来瞧瞧。只是先前公务繁忙，抽不开身。眼下难得有空，便不请自来了，诸位莫要见怪。
t_en: When I heard that the Cloudstrider's legendary chromatic echelon had arrived at the Luofu, I knew I'd have to sneak over and take a look for myself... a pity that official matters prevented me for so long. I hope my unannounced visit won't cause you any trouble.

t_ch: 不如神策府气派。
t_en: It's not as grand as the Seat of Divine Foresight.

t_ch: 哈哈，神策府的陈设初见之下确有几分雄阔肃杀的意思。但我看神策府，神策府看我，翻来覆去看上几百年，怕是都互生倦怠了。
t_en: Haha, the Seat of Divine Foresight may seem imposing at first, but having stared at it — and it back at me — for hundreds of years... we've grown rather tired of one another.

t_ch: 从这儿远眺，仿佛能洗去胸中尘埃，不觉为之一快。
t_en: It feels as if the heart and mind are wiped clean when looking into the distance from here. How refreshing.

t_ch: 对了，我此次是以私人身份前来拜会丹恒，还有列车上的诸位。若有什么我要遵守的规矩，{NICKNAME}记得告诉我。
t_en: I forgot to mention — this is a personal visit to see Dan Heng — and everyone else on the Express. If there are any rules that I should follow, do let me know, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 把这儿当家就好，将军请随意。
t_en: Just make yourself at home, general.

t_ch: 既是私人身份，还叫什么「将军」，叫我景元就好。
t_en: Haha, this is a personal visit — no need for formalities. Call me Jing Yuan.

t_ch: 以后得空，我想能常来坐坐，与各位闲谈。{NICKNAME}，你到时候该不会嫌我烦吧，哈哈！
t_en: I very much enjoy our idle conversations here... I hope to start visiting more frequently, when my schedule allows. Haha, you won't find me to be a bother, will you, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 这种松弛感，真是难得……
t_en: Such a natural state of composure is rare — even on the Xianzhou... Inspiring...

t_ch: 这种浑然天成的松弛感，就连仙舟上也不多见，真是难得……
t_en: Such a natural state of composure is rare — even on the Xianzhou... Inspiring...

t_ch: 「松弛感」？什么意思？
t_en: State of composure? What do you mean?

t_ch: 就是一种气定神闲的状态，看上去无忧无虑，但你知道对方胸有成竹，定有一番担当。
t_en: It is a state of being relaxed while maintaining a focused mind. The individual may look carefree, but they harbor a constant readiness.

t_ch: 你说的那个人是谁？
t_en: Who are you talking about?

t_ch: 那个人？哦…你是指，我方才说的有松弛感的那位？
t_en: Who? Oh... you mean the state of composure?

t_ch: 说来有些冒昧了，不过我观察了你们这位列车长许久，觉得它颇有些不凡之处。
t_en: I hope they won't take offense, but I have taken notice of your conductor — I find them to be extraordinary.

t_ch: 帕姆吗？好像是有点。
t_en: Pom-Pom? They're a little extraordinary, I guess.

t_ch: 不仅仅是「有点」。
t_en: "A little" would be an understatement.

t_ch: 它是不是特别可爱！
t_en: Isn't Pom-Pom cute!?

t_ch: 确实。不过我想，「可爱」只是它的某个侧面。
t_en: ...Yes, indeed. However, cuteness is but one of many admirable attributes.

t_ch: 列车要规划路线，检查设备情况，又要照顾诸位的起居，迎接像我这样的客人……
t_en: The Express must plan its routes, maintain its equipment, attend to the daily needs of its passengers, and receive guests, of course...

t_ch: 而看车厢这井井有条的模样，便知这些工作列车长信手拈来。它不急不慢的样子，不正是我说的「松弛感」吗？
t_en: From the orderly condition of the carriages alone, it is plain to see that the conductor is masterful in their responsibilities. Their calm demeanor is precisely the state of composure I am talking about.

t_ch: 突然对帕姆心生敬意了！
t_en: I've suddenly developed a new respect for Pom-Pom!

t_ch: 可爱的外表下兼具实力！
t_en: Cute and capable, all at once!

t_ch: 若时间允许，我想和列车长聊一聊，听它讲讲列车管理中的种种趣事。
t_en: If we have time, I would like to speak with the conductor and listen to their experiences of managing the Express.

t_ch: 若时间允许，我想和列车长聊一聊，听它讲讲列车管理中的种种趣事。
t_en: If we have time, I would like to speak with the conductor and listen to their experiences of managing the Express.

t_ch: 身为无名客，却与列车无缘……
t_en: It is a shame that, as a Nameless, her path never crossed with the Astral Express.

t_ch: 身为无名客，却与星穹列车无缘，实在可惜。要是她能登上这架列车，该多好啊……
t_en: It is a shame that, as a Nameless, her path never crossed with the Astral Express. It would be wonderful if she were aboard...

t_ch: 与列车无缘的无名客？
t_en: A Nameless whose path never crossed with the Astral Express?

t_ch: 不知镜流是否向你提起过，我们曾有位共同的故友和你们一样，也是无名客。
t_en: I'm unsure if Jingliu has ever mentioned this to you, but we had a mutual friend who was a Nameless, just like you.

t_ch: 你说的这个人是谁？
t_en: Who are you talking about?

t_ch: 一位故友，不知镜流是否向你提起过，那是许多年前的事了……
t_en: An old friend. I'm not sure if Jingliu has ever mentioned her to you — it was many years ago...

t_ch: 曜青仙舟的狐人中不乏到处探险考察的飞行世家。其中最有名的当属我这位朋友的家系。
t_en: The foxians of the Xianzhou Yaoqing are known for their adventuring flying families. The most renowned of these families was that of our mutual friend's.

t_ch: 其父母亲眷都是著作等身的学者与冒险家。她自诩承接了「无名客」的精神，以遍游星海胜景为志向，还著有一本知名游记。
t_en: Her parents were established scholars and adventurers, and she naturally inherited the spirit of the Nameless to travel and see the stars. She even has a famous travelogue in her name.

t_ch: 星穹列车的名头，我最初正是从她那儿听来的。只可惜，她游览千星，却无缘登上无名客心中的圣地，列车。
t_en: I first heard about the Astral Express from her. It is a shame that, in her travels to thousands of stars, she never had the opportunity to board the Express — the sacred place of the Nameless.

t_ch: 是我唐突了，尽说些陈年旧事，坏了气氛。
t_en: Ahem... All my talk of former days is rather spoiling the atmosphere. My apologies.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我也很期待你将来能写下你们的旅程游记，到时候，记得让我看看。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I hope you too will be able to make a travelogue of your own and include your experiences. I would love to read it.

t_ch: 我也很期待你将来能写下你们的旅程游记，到时候，记得让我看看。
t_en: I hope you too will be able to make a travelogue of your own and include your experiences. I would love to read it.

t_ch: 列车上的布设我很喜欢。
t_en: I am very fond of the furnishings on the Express.

t_ch: 列车上的布设我很喜欢。这些物件，想来是花了不少心思布置的，不知是谁的手笔？
t_en: I am very fond of the furnishings on the Express — it is apparent that whoever decorated the train truly put their heart into it. I wonder who it was?

t_ch: 大概是…帕姆？
t_en: I'm guessing it was... Pom-Pom?

t_ch: 想不到列车长的品味竟如此细腻……
t_en: I see. I am truly impressed by the attention to detail and taste of the conductor...

t_ch: 大家按各自喜好来布置的…
t_en: It was decorated by everyone according to their own preferences...

t_ch: 原来是诸位齐心协力的结果。
t_en: So this is a product of teamwork.

t_ch: 你看这沙发料子软和，若是在留声机里放一阙调子缓慢的小曲，再配上一壶鳞渊春，几块白云酥……
t_en: The fabric of the furniture is remarkably soft — I could lie here for days... All this needs is a slow, mellow tune on the phonograph, a pot of Whale-Tide Spring, and a few nimbus cakes...

t_ch: 如此高卧不起，看辰宿流转，星汉明灭，放任一天流逝。真是再美妙不过的事情了。
t_en: To be here, reclining and watching shining constellations come and go as time passes one by... There could be nothing more pleasant.

t_ch: 将军真是个享受人。
t_en: General, you really know how to have a good time.

t_ch: 都是空想而已，我没那种命呀。
t_en: Nothing but a fantasy. I was not fated for such an existence.

t_ch: 不如现在实地操作一番？
t_en: Why not give it a try right now?

t_ch: 不了不了，我还有公务在身，不克久留。
t_en: *sigh* I'm afraid there are still official matters to attend to. I must be leaving soon.

t_ch: 不过等这阵子事情忙完，我也许会来观景车厢里实践实践这样消磨光阴的法子。
t_en: However, when work does slow down, I may return to the parlor car to put such leisure into practice.

t_ch: 每当身处这里，总有种放下一切的快乐。
t_en: Whenever I step foot aboard the Express, I feel the joy of being relieved of all of my burdens.

t_ch: 每当身处这里，总有种放下一切的快乐。
t_en: Whenever I step foot aboard the Express, I feel the joy of being relieved of all of my burdens.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，不知丹恒近来在列车上过得如何？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, tell me, has Dan Heng been enjoying his time on the Express?

t_ch: 他好得很。
t_en: I'd say he's having a great time.

t_ch: 他能拥有全新的生活，我也为他感到高兴。
t_en: I'm happy that he was able to find a new life.

t_ch: 将军干嘛不亲口问他？
t_en: General, why don't you ask him yourself?

t_ch: 他拥有了全新的人生，已不再是与我相熟的故友了。
t_en: He has a new life now. He is no longer the old friend I once knew.

t_ch: 持明新生，如同重归大海的游鱼，理当自由自在。而我在岸上观望足矣。
t_en: When the Vidyadhara are reborn, they are as fish that have returned to the ocean... it is a state of complete freedom. That I may glance upon him from the shore is enough for me.

t_ch: 那么，未来的路上，丹恒就要多蒙列车团的各位照顾了。
t_en: I hope that the Express crew will look after Dan Heng.

t_ch: 我们是伙伴，应该的！
t_en: Of course. We're companions!

t_ch: 「伙伴」…好久没听到这个词了，哈哈，有同路人相伴，真好啊。
t_en: Companions... I have not heard that word in a very long time. Haha, how auspicious to be in the company of those on the same road.

t_ch: 是我们多靠他照顾了！
t_en: He's the one that usually takes care of us!

t_ch: 哈哈，在这一点上，他倒是从来也没变过。
t_en: Haha, well, I suppose that part of him has remained the same.

t_ch: 未来在列车前行的路上若是遇到了难处，别忘了罗浮永远是诸位的避风港。
t_en: If the Express ever runs into trouble in its future endeavors, please remember that the Luofu will always be a sanctuary for you all.

t_ch: 不知丹恒近来如何……
t_en: I wonder what Dan Heng's getting up to lately...

t_ch: 未来在列车前行的路上若是遇到了难处，别忘了罗浮永远是诸位的避风港。
t_en: If the Express ever runs into trouble in its future endeavors, please remember that the Luofu will always be a sanctuary for you all.

t_ch: 我为列车带了份礼物。
t_en: It is Xianzhou etiquette that as a guest, I come bearing gifts.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来得正好。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're just in time.

t_ch: 有幸受各位之邀登上列车，不可失了礼数。
t_en: I wish to express my gratitude for the invitation to the Express.

t_ch: 听闻你对这类深空中的「石头」情有独钟。所以我特地搜集了一些，当作礼物，以表心意。
t_en: I hear that you are very interested in these deep space stones. By way of thanks, I've taken it upon myself to gather some for you.

t_ch: 我不是收藏家，不知这些石头的成色你可满意？
t_en: I'm not much of a collector. I hope you find the quality of these stones satisfactory.

t_ch: 我不是收藏家，不知这些石头的成色你可满意？
t_en: I'm not much of a collector. I hope you find the quality of these stones satisfactory.

t_ch: 真是惬意呀。
t_en: It's so peaceful here...

t_ch: 突然感觉一点点紧张…难道我背后，是、是有人在看着我吗……
t_en: ...too peaceful... Why do I feel like someone's watching me...?

t_ch: 藿藿，欢迎来到星穹列车。
t_en: Welcome to the Astral Express, Huohuo.

t_ch: 呜啊，原来是{NICKNAME}呀。这让我稍稍松了一口气。
t_en: Ah! Oh, it's you, {NICKNAME}... That's a relief.

t_ch: （模仿恐怖的鬼叫声）
t_en: (Scary ghost noises)

t_ch: 呜啊啊啊啊——！救命啊！
t_en: Ahhhhhhhh! Help me! Help me!

t_ch: …原来是{NICKNAME}呀，请不要从背后吓唬我。
t_en: ...Oh it's you, {NICKNAME}. Please don't sneak up on me like that!

t_ch: 星穹列车又气派又安静，只是在观景车厢待了一会，我就感觉到了安心感，这里真好啊。
t_en: The Astral Express is so peaceful and majestic. I've only been in the Parlor Car a few moments, and I already feel calmer. This place is the best!

t_ch: 欢迎来列车做客，记得多拍照。
t_en: Welcome aboard the Astral Express. Remember to take lots of pictures.

t_ch: …好、好主意。
t_en: ...G—Good idea.

t_ch: 欢迎藿藿，记得尝尝姬子咖啡。
t_en: Welcome, Huohuo. Remember to try Miss Himeko's coffee.

t_ch: …好、好主意。
t_en: ...G—Good idea.

t_ch: 列车可真是个好地方，以后我再来这里做客时，请记得不要从背面和我打招呼。
t_en: The Express is such a lovely place... Umm, next time I visit, please... remember not to sneak up on me from behind.

t_ch: 好像有看不见的东西…
t_en: I feel an invisible presence...

t_ch: 我、我听列车长说，列车里好像有过什么看不见的东西…我便仔细观察了一下。
t_en: Th—The conductor said there might be something invisible on the Express... so, I've been trying to investigate.

t_ch: 有什么观察结果吗？
t_en: Have you sensed anything?

t_ch: …有，但好像没有，目前还不是很确定。
t_en: ...I'm not quite sure yet.

t_ch: 我好像也遇见过…
t_en: I think I encountered it...

t_ch: …原来这不是假的吗？我、我的腿好像有点站不住了……
t_en: ...Wait, r—really? Uh-oh, m—my legs are feeling a bit wobbly...

t_ch: 我还以为你肯定会逃走…
t_en: You're not running away?

t_ch: …列车上的大家对我很好，我打算帮上忙之后再逃走。
t_en: Everyone on the Express has been so nice to me... I'm only running away after I've helped out...

t_ch: 根据判官的专业判断，无形之物…或者说不祥之物就在列车里，就、就在我的正前方。
t_en: There was a moment when I felt the invisible thing was r—right in front of me...

t_ch: …但是，后来我又探测了好几遍，那里真的是什么都没有…不、不会真的是什么太空幽灵吧？我来之前，没、没有想到列车会有这么可怕……
t_en: I used all the tricks in the judges' handbook, but couldn't get any evidence. I—I—It's not some sort of space ghost, is it? I—I didn't think the Express would be this scary!

t_ch: 确实有人，就在那边！
t_en: There's actually someone right over there!

t_ch: …真、真的吗？！
t_en: ...R—Really!?

t_ch: 呜呜呜啊啊啊啊啊——！
t_en: Ahhhhhhhh!

t_ch: 那里什么都没有，是你想多了。
t_en: I'm sure it's nothing — just peoples' imaginations...

t_ch: …你怎么有种「认真骗人」的感觉？
t_en: ...I can't tell if you're serious...

t_ch: 那里肯定有问题，呜呜呜啊啊啊啊啊——！
t_en: Wait. I—I can feel it again! Ahhhhhhhh!

t_ch: …今天的列车之旅就到此为止了。唔……
t_en: ...I think that's enough Express for today.

t_ch: …今天的列车之旅就到此为止了。下、下次还有机会的话，我会再来的。
t_en: ...That's enough Express for today. The n—next time I have a chance, I'll come back and visit again.

t_ch: 我听列车长说……
t_en: I heard the conductor say...

t_ch: 我听列车长说，你在黑塔空间站遭遇意外，然后突然就踏上了星际旅途？
t_en: I heard the conductor say something happened on Herta Space Station... and then without meaning to, you found yourself on a journey through the stars.

t_ch: …照这么说来，我和你有些相似。
t_en: I feel like, um, we're kind of... similar, I guess?

t_ch: 你也是遭遇意外，进入十王司…
t_en: You mean because you didn't mean to join the Ten-Lords Commission...?

t_ch: …没错，我和尾巴大爷相遇是意外，进入十王司也是意外，擢升判官…也是意外。
t_en: ...Yeah, joining the Commission was kind of an accident... So was meeting Mr. Tail. Even getting promoted...

t_ch: 你也曾遭遇意外，乘上了列车？
t_en: ...So you didn't mean to board the Express?

t_ch: …好像…也没有那么相似？
t_en: Not exactly...

t_ch: 我是说，我和尾巴大爷相遇是意外，进入十王司也是意外，擢升判官…也是意外。
t_en: What I mean is, I've had plenty of accidents: Meeting Mr. Tail, joining the Ten-Lords Commission, even getting promoted wasn't intentional...

t_ch: 雪衣大人勾摄镇伏要犯，执行机要任务；寒鸦大人梦占因果罪愆，书写业报判罚…但是我呢，我是害怕岁阳却要抓捕岁阳的胆小鬼……
t_en: Madam Xueyi catches criminals and executes secret missions. Madam Hanya uses dreams to predict karmic offenses and write judgements... but what about me? I'm just a scaredy-cat who's afraid of heliobi... and yet it's my job to catch them...

t_ch: …有时我会想，一切如果都是巧合与意外的话，我是不是…没有判官的资格呢？
t_en: ...Sometimes I wonder: If everything that's happened to me is just a coincidence or an accident, then... am I really qualified to be a judge?

t_ch: 雪衣说你的擢升，与意外无关…
t_en: From what Xueyi said, your promotion was no accident...

t_ch: …也就是说，雪衣大人其实是认可我的能力吗？
t_en: So... you're saying Madam Xueyi has faith in my abilities?

t_ch: 虽然雪衣大人当着我的面说过，可我以为她只是在安慰我。
t_en: She did tell me that face to face... but I thought she was just trying to comfort me...

t_ch: 现在才想这个，已经来不及了…
t_en: It's too late to doubt yourself.

t_ch: …我明白你的意思——既然我已经是判官了，与其烦恼有没有资格，不如做好手边的工作。
t_en: ...Mm, I know what you mean... I'm already a judge after all. Instead of worrying about whether I'm qualified, I should just focus on doing a good job.

t_ch: 踏上列车旅行，是我的必然。
t_en: Boarding the Express was my destiny.

t_ch: 欸？是这样吗？
t_en: Oh, is that right?

t_ch: …当上十王司的判官，说不定也是我的必然。
t_en: ...Then perhaps being a Ten-Lords Commission judge is also my destiny.

t_ch: 谢谢你，我好像有所收获……
t_en: Thank you, things are a little clearer now...

t_ch: 不过，要是工作太艰难了，我、我还是会考虑申请离职的……
t_en: But, um, if work gets too tough, I might still consider quitting...

t_ch: 我有点小烦恼想咨询……
t_en: I need some advice...

t_ch: 请问{NICKNAME}方便吗，我有一点小烦恼想咨询一下。
t_en: Excuse me, {NICKNAME}, do you have time? I need some advice...

t_ch: 有什么烦恼，不妨说说看。
t_en: What's the problem? I'm all ears.

t_ch: 最近十王司工作不太忙，然后我有了一段小假期。原本我下定决心告诉自己「假期来之不易，每一天都要充实地度过」。
t_en: Lately, work at the Ten-Lords Commission isn't too busy, and I ended up with a little vacation time. At first, I told myself: "breaks are hard to come by, I should make the most of every day."

t_ch: 结果回过神来，我在家里待了三天，和平常做着一样的事情，然后假期就要结束了…不知道素裳和桂乃芬假期里在做些什么呢……
t_en: But then, before I knew it, I'd spent three days at home doing the usual things, and now my vacation is about to end... I can't help wondering what Sushang and Guinaifen are doing on their days off...

t_ch: 所以，你就来到列车上了吗？
t_en: Is that why you decided to visit the Express?

t_ch: 我、我只是觉得自己应该出门走走，就、就来到了这里。
t_en: I—I just thought it'd be good to get out... so here I am.

t_ch: 试着主动联系下她们俩如何？
t_en: Why don't you get in touch with them?

t_ch: …假期里，我内心挣扎过几次，最后都没好意思问出来。
t_en: ...I did think about that, but in the end, I never found the courage to ask them...

t_ch: 假期也不一定非得做些什么…
t_en: It's fine to do nothing while on vacation...

t_ch: …要是什么都不做的话，总觉得内心有一种虚无感。
t_en: ...If I don't do anything, I always feel a bit... empty inside.

t_ch: …我还是想找她们俩一起玩。
t_en: I'd like to hang out with them, it's just...

t_ch: 不、不过，要是由我去邀请的话，我还得安排时间地点，我肯定安排不好……
t_en: If it's up to me to do the inviting, that means I have to arrange a time and place, and I'm sure I won't do a good job of that...

t_ch: 就来列车上玩吧！
t_en: Why not invite them to the Express?

t_ch: …好、好主意。
t_en: ...G—Good idea.

t_ch: 社恐真是太辛苦了。
t_en: Social anxiety can be tough sometimes.
*sigh*

t_ch: …让我再稍稍纠结一下吧。
t_en: ...I need a bit more time to think this over.

t_ch: 假期难得，可千万不能浪费掉。我还得问问她们俩，我…我应该可以的。
t_en: Vacation days are precious. I really shouldn't waste them. I need to talk to them both. I... I should be able to manage that.

t_ch: 列车组的人真好。
t_en: The Express Crew are so nice.

t_ch: 确实不错。
t_en: They're pretty good.

t_ch: 你来啦，刚才我和尾巴大爷正在说，列车上的人很热情，甚至给尾巴准备了「茶点」。
t_en: Oh, you're here! Mr. Tail and I were just talking about how warm and welcoming the people on the Express are. You guys even prepared some snacks for him.

t_ch: 唔，不错不错，大爷我很满意。
t_en: Mm, tasty morsels. Very satisfying.

t_ch: 这就是列车的待客之道。
t_en: That's Express hospitality for you.

t_ch: 能考虑到岁阳的口味，准备好相位灵火的烧灰作为茶点，你们的列车长还算有些经验。
t_en: The fact that your conductor knew to cater to the heliobus' tastes with Phase Flame ash says a lot about their experience.

t_ch: 尾巴大爷可真是稀客呀。
t_en: You're quite the infrequent visitor, Mr. Tail.

t_ch: …什么稀客，这小姑娘溜上列车的时候，老子哪次没有在？真是的。
t_en: ...Infrequent visitor? I'm here every time this little whippersnapper is! Honestly...

t_ch: 得亏你们列车长准备了相位灵火的烧灰作为茶点，大爷我今天心情好，就不计较了。
t_en: Luckily for you, your conductor had the foresight to prepare some Phase Flame ash as a snack. I'm in a good mood today, so I'll let it slide.

t_ch: 原本刚登上列车的时候，还以为列车长只是一个吉祥物，来这里的机会多了以后，我发现列车长待客又周到又靠谱，太可爱了……
t_en: When I first boarded the Express, I thought the conductor was just a mascot... but now that I've been here a few times, I know how important they are to the train. They're so considerate and reliable.

t_ch: …对了{NICKNAME}，你觉得列车长和尾巴大爷谁更可爱？
t_en: Who do you think's cuter, {NICKNAME}? Pom-Pom or Mr. Tail?

t_ch: 哈？！不准说老子可爱！
t_en: Cuter!? I'll show you cuter!

t_ch: 帕姆更可爱。
t_en: Pom-Pom's cuter.

t_ch: 这就对喽。
t_en: Right answer.

t_ch: 帕姆看上去就很好捏！
t_en: Pom-Pom's cheeks are so pinchable!

t_ch: 嗯嗯，我懂。
t_en: Hee-hee, I know.

t_ch: 帕姆给了我很多奖励！
t_en: Pom-Pom's given me so many rewards!

t_ch: 原来如此。
t_en: Oh. Really?

t_ch: 尾巴更可爱。
t_en: Mr. Tail is cuter.

t_ch: 噫。
t_en: Bah!

t_ch: 咦，怎么啦？
t_en: You think so?

t_ch: 有一种坏坏的感觉。
t_en: There's something mischievous about him.

t_ch: 哦！原来如此。
t_en: Oh! I see what you mean.

t_ch: 我这个人喜新厌旧。
t_en: Maybe it's because he's a new face around here.

t_ch: 没想到竟然是这样的理由。
t_en: ...Uhh, I guess that makes sense...

t_ch: 很难抉择，两者都很可爱。
t_en: Tough choice — they're both very cute.

t_ch: …唔，其实我也这么觉得。
t_en: ...Hee-hee, I think so too.

t_ch: 目前，我还是认为列车长更加可爱一些。
t_en: Right now... I'd say Pom-Pom's a smidge cuter.

t_ch: 哼，老子本来就没想可爱！
t_en: Hmph, well... good!

t_ch: 我给你带了这个……
t_en: I got this for you...

t_ch: 嘿嘿～{NICKNAME}，我给你带了这个……
t_en: Hee-hee~ {NICKNAME}, I got this for you...

t_ch: 出门的时候，我去「鉴爻轩」帮你算了一下「今日运势」。若月老板说你的转运道具就是这些亮晶晶的石头，我就带来给你了。
t_en: While I was away, I stopped by The Looking Bronze and had got your "Fortune for Today." Ruoyue, the owner, said these sparkling stones were your lucky charms, so I brought them back for you. They'll bring you good fortune.

t_ch: 哇，谢谢藿藿！
t_en: Wow! Thanks, Huohuo!

t_ch: 嘿嘿～要是能转运就好了。
t_en: Hee-hee~ Hope you have good luck.

t_ch: 这怎么好意思…
t_en: You shouldn't have...

t_ch: 没事没事，你就收下吧。
t_en: It's fine, really, take them.

t_ch: 过去，我因为「贞凶之命」一直倒霉，最近我开始觉得「运势」其实并不简单。
t_en: As a cursed one, I've always been unlucky, but recently I've started to feel like there's more to fortune than meets the eye.

t_ch: 你记得吗？我第一次见到你的那天就特别倒霉，绥园闹鬼，尾巴也不见了……
t_en: On the first day we met, I was really unlucky. Fyxestroll Garden was haunted, Tail disappeared... Do you remember?

t_ch: 但同样是那一天，我认识了你，还认识了捉鬼小队，这算是好运的象征吧。
t_en: But it was also the same day I met you and got to know the ghost-hunting squad... That must be a sign of good luck, I suppose.

t_ch: 同样的事，既是凶也是吉，还真是挺奇妙的。
t_en: Bad luck and good luck, all mixed together. Funny when you think about it, right?

t_ch: 希望你拿着转运道具能有好运。
t_en: May these charms bring you good luck.

t_ch: 宁静的星空…
t_en: A serene evening...

t_ch: 我喜欢这里巨大的窗户，视野不错，可以看到宁静的星空。
t_en: Isn't it wonderful? Through these windows you can lose yourself in the peaceful starry sky.

t_ch: 星空对我们而言尤为珍贵。
t_en: The stars are precious to us.

t_ch: 是吗？我完全可以理解。走在「开拓」上…不错的体验。
t_en: Is that so? I can relate. Trailblazing across the vast unknown must be quite the experience.

t_ch: 我曾想过取一个太空的切片，用于研究星球的构成本质。
t_en: I once hoped to capture a slice of space and dissect it, to truly understand the core essence of our galaxies and planets.

t_ch: 它会是什么样的呢？
t_en: I wonder what you'd find?

t_ch: 嗯…一种透明玻璃片，透明到几乎不可见。
t_en: I imagine a slice of clarity, ethereal in its transparency.

t_ch: 一个切片可以由美丽的尘埃、气体和粒子构成，它会如同绛紫色烟雾一样叆叇不绝…绕在指尖。
t_en: Picture a sliver made up of beautiful dust, gases, and tiny specks, twirling around your finger like a captivating plum-colored mist.

t_ch: 就像戒指一样。我想，我会将它制作成与珠链般配的模样，应当散发一种温润的色泽。
t_en: It reminds me of a ring. I imagine crafting it, and then adding a bracelet of beads to complement its soft, lustrous glow.

t_ch: 欢迎随时来列车做客。
t_en: Welcome aboard the Astral Express.

t_ch: 好。希望每次，我都有新的发现。
t_en: Thank you. I hope there are new discoveries to be made each time I visit.

t_ch: 一个切片可以由美丽的尘埃、气体和粒子构成，它会如同绛紫色烟雾一样叆叇不绝…绕在指尖。
t_en: Picture a sliver made up of beautiful dust, gases, and tiny specks, twirling around your finger like a captivating plum-colored mist.

t_ch: 这样的「装饰」，{NICKNAME}你觉得如何？
t_en: This ornament — {NICKNAME}, how does it strike you?

t_ch: 绣图时…伤了手。
t_en: Got hurt while embroidering...

t_ch: 你好，{NICKNAME}。我有个不情之请…请问，你有伤药吗？
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. I hope it's not too bold of me to ask... do you happen to have any healing remedies?

t_ch: 说来有些不好意思，方才刺绣时，我伤到了手指。
t_en: It's a tad embarrassing, but I managed to prick my finger while embroidering just now.

t_ch: 有时我也会过于心急。这绣面要勾勒出花鸟绰约之姿，就要不紧不慢，时刻调整呼吸。
t_en: At times, my eagerness gets the better of me. To truly capture the grace of the birds and flowers in the embroidery, one must be patient and adjust one's breathing.

t_ch: （递出伤药）
t_en: (Give ointment)

t_ch: 谢谢你…这样清凉的触感，令疼痛也被抚平了几分。
t_en: The cool touch has alleviated some of the discomfort. It feels a bit better now.

t_ch: 唔，没有什么可回赠于你的…我身上这些小物件，都是闲暇时自己绣的。{NICKNAME}，如果你喜欢，我也可以做一些赠予你。
t_en: Hmm, I don't have much with me to offer in return... These little fancies I wear are what I embroider in my free time. {NICKNAME}, I can craft some for you too if you like.

t_ch: 设色精妙的绣物佳品，较画意更胜一筹。
t_en: Such colorful embroidery — it feels even more expressive than a painting.

t_ch: 我身上这些小物件，都是闲暇时自己绣的。{NICKNAME}，香囊或是流苏挂坠…你喜欢吗？
t_en: These little fancies I wear are what I embroider in my free time. {NICKNAME}, would you fancy a spice sachet or a tassel pendant?

t_ch: 不知道这些糕点…
t_en: Never heard of these desserts...

t_ch: 唔，现在各个星球上好像都在流行一种「海水熔岩」味的点心。据说是取了海中的水，以及苦血山脉里的熔岩。
t_en: Hmm... Everyone's talking about this "seawater magma" flavored snack — it's becoming all the rage on quite a few planets. I've heard they extract seawater and combine it with magma from the mountains.

t_ch: 虽然我觉得味道不错，但就个人喜好而言，我还是倾向于更能体现食物本味的糕点。
t_en: The taste is pleasant, but I personally have a soft spot for pastries that showcase more... organic flavors.

t_ch: 哦，对了。听说现在也很流行为之拍照，发布在通讯工具上，以此来与各个星球埠口的门店换取更多点心试吃。
t_en: Speaking of which, I heard it's become quite common to photograph these delicacies and upload them to social media... and that doing so can even earn you sample treats from different purveyors.

t_ch: 只是我对此…不太擅长。你有什么建议吗？
t_en: However, I must admit... I'm not really skilled in that area. Do you have any advice?

t_ch: 可以将其放在阳光下拍摄。
t_en: Just place them in a sunny spot and snap a few pics.

t_ch: 是吗？听起来不错。下次我试试看。
t_en: Oh, is that all? Sounds great, I'll give it a go sometime.

t_ch: 可以比一个剪刀手。
t_en: Just strike a pose with a peace sign.

t_ch: 这个动作，对我来说有些困难…但谢谢你的建议，我会再尝试一下。
t_en: That pose would present... difficulties for me, but thank you for the suggestion.

t_ch: 不知道「海水熔岩」味的糕点，你们会不会感兴趣……
t_en: I wonder if you'd be interested in seawater magma flavored pastries...

t_ch: 如果喜欢的话，下次我得多准备些。
t_en: Should you take a liking to them, I'll make sure to prepare more next time.

t_ch: 不知道「海水熔岩」味的糕点，你们会不会感兴趣……
t_en: I wonder if you'd be interested in seawater magma flavored pastries...

t_ch: 如果喜欢的话，下次我得多准备些。
t_en: Should you take a liking to them, I'll make sure to prepare more next time.

t_ch: 鲜花…似乎有些枯萎了。
t_en: Flowers... seem a bit withered.

t_ch: 列车上的鲜花，失去色泽后也会快速枯萎吧……
t_en: I imagine the flowers on the Express wither quite quickly once their vibrancy fades...

t_ch: 我正研究一种培育技术，将鲜花永久留存下来。不用制成干花，而是让水与生命永远在枝干中流动。
t_en: I'm researching a cultivation technique to preserve flowers forever... not by turning them into dried flowers, but by allowing water and life to perpetually flow within their stems...

t_ch: 但我很担心，从「短暂」变为「永久」后，是否会让鲜花不再因瞬时绽放而如此动人。就像…玫瑰被叫做其他名字后，闻起来便不再馥郁。
t_en: ...however, I'm concerned that making the ephemeral "permanent" might strip flowers of their fleeting beauty... Would a rose by any other name smell as sweet?

t_ch: 可以制作干花。
t_en: Why not produce some dried flowers?

t_ch: 可以将它的模样绣下。
t_en: How about embroidering their likeness?

t_ch: 是很好的主意。我们总有很多方式，留住它作为生命…最为美丽而夺目的时刻。
t_en: I shan't discount it. There are countless ways to immortalize the most dazzling moments of a flower's life.

t_ch: 但最令人难忘的…还是眼看着它慢慢逝去，并为此而感到心痛的那种「感受」。
t_en: But, what remains most unforgettable... is the profound sensation of watching a flower fade away and the heartache that accompanies it.

t_ch: …如果春天常在，一切就不会因终将逝去，而令人感到这般旖旎。
t_en: Without its inevitable departure, the charm of spring would diminish.

t_ch: 列车会经过…我的家乡吗？
t_en: Will the Express pass by... my home?

t_ch: 不知列车的尽头通向何方，也许会经过我的家乡也说不定呢？
t_en: I'm curious about the Express' trajectory. Will it pass by the place I call home?

t_ch: 你的家乡是什么样的？
t_en: What's your homeworld like?

t_ch: 一个锦绣之乡，那里的水很清澈。父亲是雕刻家，我与母亲一脉相承，热衷科研。母亲离世后，我应她的要求，保留了她的脑叶切片。
t_en: With its pristine waters, my homeland is like a tapestry of wonders. My father was a sculptor, but it was my mother's passion for research that I shared. Upon her passing, I held onto a piece of her brain tissue, as per her wishes.

t_ch: 但被博识尊选中后，我便离开了家乡开始研究…说起来，已很久未回去探望过父亲了。
t_en: However, after being chosen by Nous, I departed my home to delve into my research... Come to think of it, it's been quite some time since I last visited my father.

t_ch: 下次如果有空当，要回家乡看看父亲才是。
t_en: When I get a moment's respite, I ought to make a trip home to check on my father.

t_ch: 就等到…对「星神」的研究有更进一步的突破之后吧。
t_en: Perhaps I'll wait until I make further breakthroughs in my research on the Aeons.

t_ch: 列车组…与天才俱乐部？
t_en: The Crew and... the Genius Society?

t_ch: 有意思的是，不知为何，列车组的感情常常让我想起我与黑塔、螺丝咕姆和斯蒂芬的关系。
t_en: I can't explain it, but for some reason, the dynamics among the Express Crew often remind me of my relationship with Herta, Screwllum, and Stephen.

t_ch: 但事实上，我们远没有你们那样熟络…唔，看来这种联想有些无端了。
t_en: Still, in reality, we're nowhere near as close as you... Hmm, perhaps I'm drawing connections where there aren't any.

t_ch: 天才俱乐部就是这样一群人吧。
t_en: Sounds a lot like the Genius Society to me.

t_ch: 你们的友情也不赖嘛。
t_en: But you're still good friends, right?

t_ch: 算不上友情。模拟宇宙项目结束后，可能我们就会分道扬镳…感谢黑塔在空间站对我睁一只眼闭一只眼。
t_en: I might not refer to them as friends — once the Simulated Universe project is over, chances are we'll part ways... but I'm grateful to Herta for always granting me some leeway on the space station.

t_ch: 至于螺丝咕姆…是位不错的贵族。只是他所在意的领域，与我的研究目的并不相投。
t_en: As for Screwllum... he's a good-hearted noble. It's just that his area of interest doesn't quite align with my research goals.

t_ch: 斯蒂芬…这孩子十分善良，总是让人禁不住多关照他几分。
t_en: Stephen is a kind-hearted boy, always making one feel compelled to look out for him.

t_ch: 不知道下一次，天才俱乐部会员再次会面是什么时候。
t_en: Who knows when the Genius Society will have its next get-together.

t_ch: 不知道下一次，天才俱乐部会员再次会面是什么时候。
t_en: Who knows when the Genius Society will have its next get-together.

t_ch: 也许还会遇见新的同伴，但我并不知道…是否能与别人相处得很好。
t_en: Perhaps I'll encounter new friends in the meantime, but I'm not sure... I sometimes wonder whether I'm able to get along well with others.

t_ch: 光是和黑塔、螺丝咕姆和斯蒂芬相处，有时也会让我稍感疲惫。
t_en: Spending time with Herta, Screwllum, and Stephen is enough to wear one out.

t_ch: 挺意外的…
t_en: Kinda surprising...

t_ch: 不错，不错。精致的内饰，整洁的布置，环境安静，也很适合思考。
t_en: How quaint. A space so meticulously curated just for my refined tastes. I suppose it might do for moments of reflection.

t_ch: 最重要的是…这里人不少，但笨蛋却不多。
t_en: Perhaps the best part... is that even with so many present, there's a delightful scarcity of simpletons.

t_ch: 谢谢你。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 不客气。
t_en: Not at all.

t_ch: 你在骂谁？
t_en: Who are you insulting?

t_ch: 这是赞美。
t_en: That was a compliment.

t_ch: 无名客在旅途中，一定会与各方人士产生交集，这种人际交往不会让你觉得厌烦么？
t_en: As a Nameless who's always on the road, constantly meeting with various characters — don't these encounters become wearisome?

t_ch: 习惯了。
t_en: I'm used to it.

t_ch: 任务罢了。
t_en: Work is still work.

t_ch: 不容易。普通人可是极易被环境影响的。
t_en: It's no small feat. The average person is easily swayed by their environment, after all.

t_ch: 希望列车的氛围也能同化你。
t_en: Then I hope you are swayed by the Express.

t_ch: 尽管试试吧，我会戴上我的头套，不被任何人打扰。
t_en: Go ahead and try. With this mask on, I intend to keep the world at bay.

t_ch: 我会戴上我的头套，不被任何人打扰。
t_en: With this mask on, I intend to keep the world at bay.

t_ch: 听说你们的智库……
t_en: I heard the data bank...

t_ch: 在你们的智库中，知识也是以数据的形式储存。
t_en: So, within your esteemed data bank, knowledge is reduced to mere bytes and bits?

t_ch: 虽说从传播角度更为方便，但也令阅读者失去了许多乐趣。
t_en: Sure, it's handy for sharing, but it surely robs the reader of genuine pleasure, does it not?

t_ch: 我时常会怀念以前手持纸本书的时光。
t_en: The touch of a paper book is a sensation I frequently find myself missing.

t_ch: 我一点都没觉得
t_en: I can't say I do.

t_ch: 你和丹恒去说吧
t_en: You should tell Dan Heng.

t_ch: 怎么，很难理解么？你是不是从来不看书？
t_en: What, can't grasp the concept? I'd wager you haven't opened a real book in your life.

t_ch: 指尖触碰纸张的触感，翻动书页时的声响，都会在思路闭塞时给予大脑刺激，引导你一步步走出瓶颈。
t_en: The sensation of paper under one's fingertips, the sound of turning a page — they provide psychological stimulation when one's thoughts are stalled, leading you gradually out of any mental impasse.

t_ch: 于我，纸本书能令人时刻意识到知识的重量，是数据永远无法替代的感知。
t_en: To me, holding a physical book constantly reminds one of the gravity of knowledge, an experience that digital data just can't replicate.

t_ch: 如果只把纸本书视作一种载体，未免也太肤浅了。
t_en: To see paper books merely as vessels would be too simplistic.

t_ch: 对我来说，纸本书能让我时刻意识到知识的重量，这是数据永远无法替代的感知。
t_en: To me, holding a physical book constantly reminds one of the gravity of knowledge, an experience that digital data just can't replicate.

t_ch: 如果只把纸本书视作过时的信息载具，未免也太肤浅了。
t_en: To see paper books merely as vessels would be too simplistic.

t_ch: 你是哪一种人？
t_en: What kind of person are you?

t_ch: 无法下决定的人，要么想得太多，要么脑袋空空。
t_en: People who struggle with decisions are either overthinkers or lack thoughts altogether.

t_ch: 你是哪一种？
t_en: Which are you?

t_ch: 想得太多！
t_en: I'm an overthinker!

t_ch: 哦，你加入天才俱乐部了吗？如果没有——你得意什么？
t_en: So, you're part of the Genius Society? If not then what is there to boast about?

t_ch: 脑袋空空！
t_en: My head is empty!

t_ch: 敢于承认这点，你已经比大多数人有悟性了。
t_en: The fact that you can admit this already indicates you have more insight than most.

t_ch: 蠢材之所以为蠢材，正是因为无法自知。希望你不要变成那样。
t_en: What defines a fool is the inability to recognize their own shortcomings. Don't let that be you.

t_ch: 蠢材之所以为蠢材，正是因为无法自知。希望你不要变成那样。
t_en: What defines a fool is the inability to recognize their own shortcomings. Don't let that be you.

t_ch: 在面对压力的时候…
t_en: When feeling stressed...

t_ch: 在面对压力的时候，你会扪心自问么——你所做的哪些事会对自己有所帮助？
t_en: In moments of stress, do you ever ask yourself — which of my actions truly serve my own interests?

t_ch: 不找人说话就不舒服
t_en: I feel uncomfortable if I keep things bottled up.

t_ch: 如果不是为了教学与诊疗，我更愿意少说多想。
t_en: Outside of teaching and healing, I lean towards keeping my words few and my thoughts deep.

t_ch: 不多运动就不舒服
t_en: If I'm not staying active, I just don't feel right.

t_ch: 我笑了。这就是无名客？忙忙碌碌，从没停下的时候。
t_en: Hah. So this is the life of a "Nameless"? Eternally bustling about, never catching a moment's rest.

t_ch: 改变是一种痛苦，也无可避免地伴随着旧事物的消亡……
t_en: Transformation is a kind of suffering, yet it invariably accompanies the end of what once was...

t_ch: 仔细想想，你的自我是否也是如此。
t_en: Upon reflection, does this also apply to you?

t_ch: 改变是一种痛苦，也无可避免地伴随着旧事物的消亡……
t_en: Transformation is a kind of suffering, yet it invariably accompanies the end of what once was...

t_ch: 仔细想想，你的自我是否也是如此。
t_en: Upon reflection, does this also apply to you?

t_ch: 天才和庸人……
t_en: Genius and mediocrity...

t_ch: 以你所见，天才和庸人的定夺权在谁手中？
t_en: In your view, who holds the power to distinguish between genius and mediocrity?

t_ch: 博识尊？
t_en: Nous?

t_ch: 即使未被「博识尊」选中，我留下的实绩也不会失去价值。
t_en: Nous or no Nous, my contributions remain invaluable.

t_ch: 你自己？
t_en: You?

t_ch: 人确实乐于给自己贴上标签。
t_en: It's true, people love to label themselves.

t_ch: 答案是，无关紧要。
t_en: The truth is, it's of no significance.

t_ch: 做出选择，然后去背负、思考、接纳，这样便好，不需要自证什么，也不需要得到认可。
t_en: Make a choice, bear it, reflect on it, and accept it. There's no need to prove anything or seek validation.

t_ch: 答案是，无关紧要。
t_en: The truth is, it's of no significance.

t_ch: 做出选择，然后去背负、思考、接纳，这样便好，不需要自证什么，也不需要得到认可。
t_en: Make a choice, bear it, reflect on it, and accept it. There's no need to prove anything or seek validation.

t_ch: 送点什么好呢……
t_en: What gifts should I prepare...

t_ch: 多次造访星穹列车，我也理应带些谢礼。
t_en: Having visited the Express so many times, it's only fitting I bring a token of my appreciation.

t_ch: 我自知不讨人喜欢，但基本的礼节还是有的。
t_en: I recognize I may not be the most endearing person, but I still uphold fundamental courtesies.

t_ch: 你有什么想要的？
t_en: Is there something you desire?

t_ch: 免费人生对谈
t_en: Free life coaching.

t_ch: 长期技术顾问
t_en: A permanent technical consultant.

t_ch: 这不就是我平时的工作——「愚钝」是世界的顽疾，传播智慧便是在医治宇宙。
t_en: I often find myself addressing the world's seeming inclination towards ignorance. But sharing a bit of my acquired knowledge? Well, I suppose it's my modest contribution to the universe.

t_ch: 可惜，大多数人都不明白这道理…知识看似免费，却最为昂贵。
t_en: Regrettably, most languish in ignorance... They fail to grasp that while knowledge appears costless, its true value is immeasurable.

t_ch: 这不就是我平时的工作——「愚钝」是世界的顽疾，传播智慧便是在医治宇宙。
t_en: I often find myself addressing the world's seeming inclination towards ignorance. But sharing a bit of my acquired knowledge? Well, I suppose it's my modest contribution to the universe.

t_ch: 可惜，大多数人都不明白这道理…知识看似免费，却最为昂贵。
t_en: Regrettably, most languish in ignorance... They fail to grasp that while knowledge appears costless, its true value is immeasurable.

t_ch: 我不是在梦里吧……
t_en: Is this a dream...?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来了！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're here!

t_ch: 这是我第一次去往匹诺康尼之外，多么壮丽啊……
t_en: This is my first time outside of Penacony... Wow. It's so awesome.

t_ch: 和你想象中的一样吗？
t_en: Is it what you were expecting?

t_ch: 虽然已经在脑海里想象过无数遍银河的模样，但是真正看到群星的时候，还…还是被震撼了……
t_en: I imagined the cosmos in my head... but actually seeing it with my own eyes is truly... mesmerizing...

t_ch: 闭上眼睛，好像下一秒，就会抵达它们的所在。
t_en: When I shut my eyes, I get this feeling that I'm among the stars.

t_ch: 以后这样的机会还很多。
t_en: You'll have a lot more chances like this in the future.

t_ch: 好啊！只是…酒店的事务繁忙，我的假期可能不够了。
t_en: Great! It's just... the hotel is so busy, my vacation might not be long enough.

t_ch: 真想和大家一起在银河间旅行啊。
t_en: But... I really want to travel through the cosmos with you all!

t_ch: 列车的大门会一直为你敞开。
t_en: You'll always be welcome on the Express.

t_ch: 谢谢！等爷爷回来之后，米沙也许就能一起启程前往远方了！
t_en: Thank you! Once Grandpa returns, perhaps I'll get the chance to join you on adventures to far-off worlds!

t_ch: 其实，来到这里之前，我经常会梦见一辆在银河间穿行的列车。璀璨的群星在它身边掠过，在梦里，我不愿意离开，会一直一直趴在车窗上看着外面的景色。但醒来之后，它就消失了。
t_en: Actually, I often dream of a train traveling through the cosmos, with brilliant stars whizzing by. I'd always be glued to the window, gazing outside. I dreamed I'd never leave, but once I woke up, it would all be gone.

t_ch: 今天终于踏上了星穹列车，我特别高兴…感觉就像回到了家一样！
t_en: But I can't believe I finally got to come aboard the Astral Express. It almost feels like home!

t_ch: 今天终于踏上了星穹列车，我特别高兴…感觉就像回到了家一样！
t_en: Yes! I can't believe I finally got to come aboard the Astral Express. It almost feels like home!

t_ch: 哇，好复杂的构造……
t_en: Woah... it all looks so complex...

t_ch: 能够在星际之间跃迁的列车，即使外表构造看着很简洁，但内部一定相当复杂。
t_en: A train that can jump between stars... Even if it looks simple on the outside, I imagine the inside is really complicated.

t_ch: 要在漫长的旅途中提供舒适的体验，列车的保养、维修和清洁工作，也一定很辛苦吧。
t_en: I bet it's a lot of work to keep the Express running smoothly over such long distances. It must need a lot of maintenance and cleaning.

t_ch: 米沙有什么建议吗？
t_en: Do you have any suggestions?

t_ch: 得研究研究…老实说，我没有做过这么复杂的工作，还要向你们学习呢。
t_en: Hmm... Well, I'll need to look into it... Honestly, I've never worked on something so intricate. Guess I'll be learning from you guys!

t_ch: 有列车长在，一切都不是问题！
t_en: With the conductor around, everything will be just fine!

t_ch: 是一直在车厢内巡逻检查、耳朵很长的那位么…能够将庞大的星穹列车打理得井井有条，太令人佩服了！
t_en: Ah, the one with the long ears always walking around the cars? It's amazing they keep everything on the Express so well-organized!

t_ch: 「白日梦酒店」太大了，我打扫客房总是会犯错…有机会一定要向列车长讨教打理列车的方法。
t_en: Yeah, keeping up with the cleaning at "The Reverie" can be tough due to its sheer size. I wonder if the conductor might share some organizing tips with me.

t_ch: 有机会一定要向列车长讨教保持整洁的方法。
t_en: I wonder if the conductor might share some organizing tips with me.

t_ch: 真想泡在智库里一整天……
t_en: I'd love to spend a whole day in the data bank...

t_ch: 好细致的记录！看了一天智库，我差点忘了时间。
t_en: The level of detail in these records is amazing! Oops... have I really been looking at the data bank all day?

t_ch: 「开拓」的故事，总是这么令人动容……
t_en: The "Trailblazing" stories are just so moving...

t_ch: 不用着急，慢慢看。
t_en: Just take your time. There's no rush.

t_ch: 想把它们都牢牢记在脑海里，这样，不在列车上的时候也可以经常回忆。
t_en: I'm trying to capture all these moments in my mind, that way I'll have them with me even when I'm not on the Express.

t_ch: 我也喜欢「开拓」的故事。
t_en: I like the "Trailblazing" stories, too.

t_ch: 孤绝的世界，星海间的银轨，充满未知的冒险之旅…太让人向往了。
t_en: Shimmering trails among the stars, boundless adventures... How wondrous...

t_ch: 可惜「开拓」的星神阿基维利陨落了，「开拓」的旅途也受到了阻塞。
t_en: It's a shame that the fall of Akivili has obstructed many Trailblazing paths.

t_ch: 我们仍然追随着阿基维利。
t_en: Nevertheless, we still follow the steps of Akivili.

t_ch: 是的！「无名客」就是我心中英雄的模样，也是我想要追随的榜样。
t_en: Exactly! For me, the Nameless represent the ideal hero. I hope to follow in their footsteps.

t_ch: 我们依然行走在路上。
t_en: We are still walking on the path.

t_ch: 嗯，虽然暂时还不能与你们同行，米沙会默默为你们祝福！
t_en: Although I can't come along with you all, I'll be here, wishing you the best!

t_ch: 旅途总是充满了艰难险阻。
t_en: Adventures always seem to come with their own set of risks.

t_ch: 嗯，这些困难打不倒我们。爷爷说过，困难和挑战是成为英雄必须要经历的事情。
t_en: That's right. Grandpa always said that facing difficulties is part of becoming a hero.

t_ch: 从我第一次听到「无名客」故事的时候，我就对星空产生了深深的向往。
t_en: I've been deeply fascinated by the starry skies ever since I first heard the story of the Nameless.

t_ch: 「开拓」的故事，总是这么令人动容……
t_en: The "Trailblazing" stories are just so moving...

t_ch: 登上列车的客人……
t_en: Every guest who comes aboard...

t_ch: 看到列车上有客房车厢，星穹列车也会像白日梦酒店一样迎接很多客人吗？
t_en: Oh, hey! I noticed there are Passenger Cabins on the Express. Does the Express welcome many guests, too, like The Reverie?

t_ch: 没错，我也曾是「客人」之一。
t_en: Yep, I was also a guest at one point.

t_ch: 对…对哦！你已经是列车上的一员了…能认识新的伙伴，就是旅途的意义。
t_en: Oh, that's right! You're already a part of the Express... Making new friends is what it's all about.

t_ch: 你是指「乘员」吗？
t_en: You mean a part of the Crew?

t_ch: 对…对哦！你已经不是客人了。能在旅途上认识新的伙伴，真好啊。
t_en: That's right, you're no longer just a guest! Making new friends is what it's all about.

t_ch: 想加入星穹列车吗？
t_en: Want to join the Astral Express Crew?

t_ch: 真的吗？可以吗？
t_en: Really? Can I?

t_ch: 不过得先等爷爷回家…在那之前我要认真工作，快快变成靠谱的大人。
t_en: Oh, well... I should wait for Grandpa to come home first... I'll keep pushing myself, and eventually, I'll become a dependable adult.
